reviews.title: Beautiful family experience
reviews.positives:
We stayed at Pullman Phu Quoc for the second time this February as a family with two young children, and overall, our experience once again confirmed why we enjoy this property.

Breakfast

The breakfast is genuinely one of the best on the island. The buffet offers a wide variety of high-quality options, everything is fresh, beautifully presented, and constantly replenished. The service team is attentive and proactive — coffee arrives quickly, staff guide you to tables, and overall the atmosphere is welcoming and efficient.

Guests also have the option to enjoy breakfast at Mad Cow restaurant for a small additional charge with à la carte options, which is also an excellent experience.

The only minor observation regarding the buffet is something that may actually concern the hotel more than guests: there appears to be limited verification of who enters the breakfast area. On several occasions we noticed individuals who were clearly not hotel guests accessing the buffet. While this did not directly impact our enjoyment, it may be something worth reviewing from an operational perspective.

One small note — the omelette station staff member (Khoa) sometimes appeared slightly reluctant in his interactions. However, this was an isolated observation, and overall the breakfast team is excellent.

⸻

Family Comfort & Facilities

For families with children, the hotel is very convenient. The pool area is well maintained, the buggy service is fast and efficient, and moving around the property is very comfortable.

The resort grounds are beautifully maintained — it is clear that a great deal of effort, time, and care is invested in landscaping and overall presentation. The property feels vibrant, well-kept, and thoughtfully designed.

⸻

Beach Experience

reviews.title: A place I want to go again.
reviews.text: I am a university student traveling with a friend! I fell in love with the accommodation after seeing the beautiful pool photos, and indeed, the pool was very pretty and well-maintained. (The staff frequently walked around, scooping floating branches with a net and cleaning here and there.) However, the breakfast was so delicious that when I tried to wear my bikini, my belly ended up sticking out! Definitely try the mango bingsu; it was the tastiest mango I've had among the ones I bought from the market, the street vendor, and the supermarket. The private beach is also beautifully arranged, and you must watch the sunset here. I still can't forget that happy moment. The only downside is the location, which is a bit ambiguous. While it's close to the airport, it's far from downtown, far from VinWonders, and quite a distance from the south, making Grab essential. There’s nothing around, not even a supermarket or a pharmacy, just barren land. Of course, if your main goal is a hotel vacation, I highly recommend it. My friend and I talked a lot about wanting to come back around this time next year and wanting to come with family too.

reviews.title: It's great for relaxing!
reviews.text: As soon as you arrive, friendly staff greet you. You can get a good pickup from the airport, and the welcome juice is delicious! Early check-in and late check-out were both free thanks to my Accor membership, and they took care of it on the same day. The hotel is close to the airport and the Sonasi night market, but you need to use Grab or a shuttle to get there! Using the hotel's night market shuttle bus and the VinBus allowed me to travel without transportation costs, so I ended up using Grab less than I expected. The breakfast changed a little over the three days I stayed, and the mango bingsu is indeed delicious. You can rent tubes and life jackets in the pool, and boards are also available for free at the beach, making it a great place to have fun! I was able to enjoy a beautiful sunset from my room. It's a hotspot for sunset views.

reviews.title: Best Resort Ever!!
reviews.text: Pullman Resort was an hour away from VinWonders and about a 20min motorbike ride to the middle of the island where the locals were. We stayed here for 4 days and 3 nights; it was an amazing experience. They made us feel very special and was accommodating- especially Chi (Bartender from the bar inside the hotel) she made us laugh and was very kind, Dat (Chicken), Khang, and there was another person he worked at the front of the hotel- helping us with transportation to the airport and renting motorbikes! The hospitality was incredible and this was by far the best one we’ve experienced! We plan to come back with the future with our family and show them the same :) we also stayed in the Cabana which had our own private pool access and was far from the main hotel at all. About a 3 minute walk- we later discovered shortcuts. I’m so happy we stayed here and so thankful of the amazing staff that made our trip very memorable! I would recommended renting the motorbikes, since the hotel was in an area where it wasn’t crowded, riding the bikes made it very easy and fun :) we took it to the middle of the island and enjoyed going around trying different foods. Thank you so much Phu Quoc Pullman!

reviews.title: Gorgeous resort on a lovely beach.
reviews.positives:
Beautiful property with a gorgeous pool and surrounding gardens. 

The food was also very high quality. Mostly, the staff were very attentive.
reviews.negatives:
This could so easily have been a 10/10, apart from 2 things.

1. Whilst there the resort was hosting a large corporate event which, on the whole, didn’t impact us……… until the last day. Whilst relaxing on our sun-bed on the last day of our stay, the entire contingent of delegates descended onto the beach for a noisy team building event. It was so raucous that one of the delegates came up to me and sincerely apologised for ruining our relaxation. Not his fault at all, but we do feel the guests should have been warned as we felt pushed off the beach. We did message to complain via this app but got no response! 

2. Upon checking out, the collection of bags and getting them to our taxi was something of a mess. We called for help with the cases, but it took so long for anyone to turn up that we started making our own way with the cases before being intercepted half way. Ok, so these things happen, and not the end of the world. So now we checked out and informed the concierge that our taxi was imminent and we would need the cases taking outside. This didn’t happen, and the taxi driver had to enter the hotel and push the trolley to load up the cases, very kind of him. I personally feel this all happened because of one particular member of the concierge who was very busy walking around looking busy…… but not actually doing anything than being over dramatic.

This is a bit of a shame as the Pullman is an amazing resort with wonderful staff. We were really happy there other than these 2 things.

Book an extra bed while booking.
